qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Global member can read secret-bearing log-streaming destination configs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a member-readable log-streaming destination disclosure on GET /eventbus/destination in log-streaming.ee, not the advisory's name-based generic HTTP credential resolution plus permission-check bypass during workflow execution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in the session identifies the advisory-described credential-theft chain rooted in name-based generic HTTP credential resolution and the credential permission-check bypass.
evidence: The advisory describes plaintext secret theft from other users' generic HTTP credentials (httpBasicAuth, httpHeaderAuth, httpQueryAuth) by resolving another user's credential ID and executing a workflow without proper authorization.

The only qualifying finding targets GET /eventbus/destination and code under packages/@n8n/permissions/src/roles/scopes/global-scopes.ee.ts and packages/cli/src/modules/log-streaming.ee/*, where member-visible destination serialization discloses stored log-streaming webhook headers, query secrets, or Sentry DSNs.

The finding report and exploit path do not involve generic HTTP credential types, workflow execution, name-based credential lookup, or a pre-execution credential permission checker bypass, and the affected code is an .ee log-streaming feature rather than the Community Edition credential pipeline named by the advisory.
